 {"id":"bd-12k","title":"Add console re-exports to sqlmodel prelude","description":"## Purpose
Add all necessary console types and traits to the sqlmodel prelude for easy access by users.

## Background
Users should be able to get started with:
use sqlmodel::prelude::*;

And have access to all commonly-used console types without additional imports.

## Implementation Details

### File Modifications
crates/sqlmodel/src/lib.rs
crates/sqlmodel/src/prelude.rs (create if not exists)

### Types to Re-export

#### Core Console Types
pub use sqlmodel_console::{
    SqlModelConsole,
    OutputMode,
    Theme,
    ConsoleAware,
};

#### Renderables (commonly used)
pub use sqlmodel_console::renderables::{
    ErrorPanel,
    QueryResultTable,
    SchemaTree,
    TableInfo,
    MigrationStatus,
};

#### Progress Components
pub use sqlmodel_console::progress::{
    OperationProgress,
    IndeterminateSpinner,
    BatchOperationTracker,
    PoolStatusDisplay,
};

### Conditional Export
Only export when console feature is enabled:
#[cfg(feature = "console")]
pub use sqlmodel_console::*;

### Prelude Module Structure
pub mod prelude {
    // Core types always available
    pub use crate::{Model, Session, Query, ...};

    // Console types when feature enabled
    #[cfg(feature = "console")]
    pub use crate::console::*;
}

## Verification Steps
1. use sqlmodel::prelude::* compiles with console feature
2. use sqlmodel::prelude::* compiles without console feature
3. All commonly-used types accessible
4. No naming conflicts with existing prelude items
5. Documentation shows available types

## Dependencies
- sqlmodel-console crate complete
- All renderable types implemented","acceptance_criteria":"Prelude re-exports SqlModelConsole when console feature enabled
Prelude re-exports OutputMode and Theme
Prelude re-exports all renderable types
Re-exports are feature-gated correctly
No naming conflicts with existing exports
All unit tests verify re-exports","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:14:23.840818971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:04:24.255562683Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:04:24.255518700Z","close_reason":"Added console re-exports to sqlmodel prelude - compiles with and without console feature","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-12k","depends_on_id":"bd-318","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-12k","depends_on_id":"bd-vz2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"bd-12k","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Required Unit Tests

1. test_prelude_exports_core_types - verify SqlModelConsole, OutputMode, Theme exported
2. test_prelude_exports_renderables - verify ErrorPanel, QueryResultTable, etc. exported
3. test_prelude_exports_progress - verify progress components exported
4. test_prelude_without_console_feature - verify compiles without console feature
5. test_prelude_no_naming_conflicts - verify no conflicts with existing prelude items
6. test_cfg_feature_gating - verify types only available when feature enabled
7. test_glob_import_usage - verify 'use sqlmodel::prelude::*' works correctly","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:30:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-18z","title":"Phase 9: Testing Infrastructure and Quality Assurance","description":"## Purpose
Establish comprehensive testing for all console components, ensuring correctness, agent compatibility, visual quality, and performance.

## Background
Testing console output requires special approaches:
- Unit tests for logic and structure
- Visual tests for rendered output
- Agent compatibility tests (parsing stdout/stderr)
- Performance tests (output must not slow operations)
- Feature flag tests (with/without console feature)

## Key Deliverables

### 1. Unit Test Suite
- Theme parsing and color generation
- OutputMode detection logic
- Renderable construction and configuration
- Progress calculation (rates, ETAs)
- Error message formatting

### 2. Visual Example Programs
- examples/console_demo.rs - showcase all renderables
- examples/error_showcase.rs - error panel variations
- examples/query_results.rs - table formatting
- examples/progress_demo.rs - progress indicators
- examples/schema_visualization.rs - tree and table info

### 3. Agent Compatibility Tests
- Verify stdout contains only semantic data
- Verify stderr contains decorative output
- Test plain mode produces parseable text
- Test agent detection via environment variables
- Verify no escape codes in plain mode

### 4. Performance Benchmarks
- Measure overhead of console output
- Compare with/without console enabled
- Benchmark progress bar update rate
- Ensure console does not become bottleneck

### 5. Feature Flag Matrix Tests
- Test compilation with all feature combinations
- Test runtime behavior with/without features
- Verify no compile errors in any combination

## Test Organization
tests/
  console/
    unit/
      theme_test.rs
      mode_detection_test.rs
      renderables_test.rs
    integration/
      agent_compat_test.rs
      stream_separation_test.rs
    visual/
      snapshot_test.rs

## Dependencies
- All console components complete (Phases 1-8)
- Example database for integration tests

## Verification
- All tests pass
- Code coverage > 80% for console crate
- Visual examples produce expected output
- Agent compatibility verified","acceptance_criteria":"Unit test suite has >80% code coverage
E2E test suite exercises all console features
Agent compatibility tests verify Plain mode behavior
Visual example programs demonstrate all features
Performance benchmarks measure console overhead
Test fixtures provide consistent sample data
All tests pass in CI environment","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:15:09.913125857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T07:00:18.344986862Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T07:00:18.344860136Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-18z","depends_on_id":"bd-318","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-18z","depends_on_id":"bd-eqb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2,"issue_id":"bd-18z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Unit test suite with >80% coverage
- [ ] Visual example programs for all renderables
- [ ] Agent compatibility tests pass
- [ ] Performance benchmarks show acceptable overhead
- [ ] E2E test suite with detailed logging passes
- [ ] Test fixtures provide consistent sample data
- [ ] All tests run successfully in CI","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:37:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1a2","title":"Create sqlmodel-console crate directory structure","description":"# Create sqlmodel-console Crate Directory Structure

## Task Description

Create the directory structure for the new `sqlmodel-console` crate following the 
standard layout used by other crates in the workspace.

## Directory Structure to Create

```
crates/sqlmodel-console/
├── Cargo.toml
└── src/
    ├── lib.rs
    ├── mode.rs
    ├── console.rs
    ├── theme.rs
    ├── renderables/
    │   └── mod.rs
    └── widgets/
        └── mod.rs
```

## Implementation Steps

1. Create the `crates/sqlmodel-console/` directory
2. Create `crates/sqlmodel-console/src/` directory
3. Create `crates/sqlmodel-console/src/renderables/` directory
4. Create `crates/sqlmodel-console/src/widgets/` directory
5. Create placeholder files with minimal content (module declarations)

## Initial File Contents

### lib.rs (minimal)
```rust
//! SQLModel Console - Beautiful terminal output for sqlmodel_rust
//!
//! This crate provides styled console output that automatically adapts to
//! the terminal environment. When running under an AI coding agent, output
//! is plain text. When running interactively, output is richly formatted.

#![forbid(unsafe_code)]

pub mod mode;
pub mod console;
pub mod theme;
pub mod renderables;
pub mod widgets;
```

### mode.rs, console.rs, theme.rs (minimal)
```rust
//! [Module description]
// Implementation in subsequent tasks
```

### renderables/mod.rs, widgets/mod.rs (minimal)
```rust
//! [Module description]
// Renderables/widgets added in later phases
```

## Verification

```bash
ls -la crates/sqlmodel-console/
ls -la crates/sqlmodel-console/src/
ls -la crates/sqlmodel-console/src/renderables/
ls -la crates/sqlmodel-console/src/widgets/
```

## Notes

- Do NOT add to workspace Cargo.toml yet (separate task)
- Placeholder content is fine; real implementation comes in subsequent tasks
- Follow existing crate structure patterns from sqlmodel-core, sqlmodel-query, etc.","acceptance_criteria":"Directory crates/sqlmodel-console/ created
src/ subdirectory exists
src/lib.rs exists with basic structure
renderables/ subdirectory exists
widgets/ subdirectory exists
All directories have appropriate permissions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:02:47.813952663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:09:54.019349682Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T09:09:54.018573540Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-1","rich-rust","setup"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1a2","depends_on_id":"bd-1vz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1lh","title":"Implement BatchOperationTracker for bulk database operations","description":"## Purpose
Create a specialized tracker for bulk database operations that tracks batches, rows affected, errors, and calculates rates automatically.

## Background
Bulk operations like batch inserts, updates, or data migrations have specific tracking needs:
- Batch-level progress (batch 5 of 20)
- Row-level progress (2500 rows inserted)
- Error counting and threshold warnings
- Automatic rate calculation
- Summary statistics at completion

## Implementation Details

### File Location
crates/sqlmodel-console/src/renderables/batch_tracker.rs

### Core Struct
BatchOperationTracker holds:
- operation_name: String
- total_batches: u64
- completed_batches: u64
- total_rows: u64
- processed_rows: u64
- error_count: u64
- started_at: Instant
- batch_times: Vec<Duration> (for rate smoothing)
- theme: Theme

### Rendering (Rich Mode)
Two-line display:
Line 1: Batch progress bar
Line 2: Row count, rate, errors

Batch insert [=========>        ] 50% (10/20 batches)
  Rows: 5,000/10,000 | Rate: 523 rows/s | Errors: 0

### Rate Calculation
- Track last N batch durations for smoothed rate
- rows_per_second = rows_in_recent_batches / duration_of_recent_batches
- Avoids spiky rates from single fast/slow batches

### Error Highlighting
- 0 errors: green or hidden
- 1+ errors: yellow with count
- Errors > threshold (configurable): red with warning

### Plain Text (Agent Mode)
Batch insert: 50% (10/20 batches), 5000/10000 rows, 523 rows/s, 0 errors

### Completion Summary
When all batches complete, show summary panel:
- Total time
- Total rows
- Average rate
- Error count
- Success rate percentage

## API Design
BatchOperationTracker::new(name, total_batches, total_rows)
  .theme(theme)
  .error_threshold(10)  // warn when errors exceed this
  .complete_batch(rows_in_batch)
  .record_error()
  .render(width) / .render_plain()
  .render_summary()  // final summary

## Verification Steps
1. Test normal batch completion
2. Test with errors
3. Test error threshold warning
4. Verify rate calculation accuracy
5. Test completion summary
6. Verify plain text format
7. Test with single batch (edge case)

## Dependencies
- OperationProgress (reuse progress bar logic)
- Theme from this crate
- std::time for timing","acceptance_criteria":"BatchOperationTracker shows overall batch progress
Tracker shows individual item status within batch
Tracker handles cancellation gracefully
Plain mode outputs batch status updates
All unit tests verify batch tracking
Performance tests confirm acceptable overhead for large batches","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:12:52.556586848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:28:47.633125588Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:28:47.633082677Z","close_reason":"Implemented BatchOperationTracker with rate smoothing, error tracking, and 26 unit tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lh","depends_on_id":"bd-1q2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1lh","depends_on_id":"bd-3gj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-1lh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests to Implement

1. test_batch_tracker_creation - verify initial state
2. test_batch_complete - verify batch counting
3. test_batch_rows_tracking - verify row accumulation
4. test_batch_rate_calculation - verify throughput
5. test_batch_error_recording - verify error count
6. test_batch_error_threshold - verify warning trigger
7. test_batch_render_plain - verify text format
8. test_batch_summary - verify completion summary
9. test_batch_single_batch - edge case
10. test_batch_many_batches - stress test","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:27:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1lv","title":"Implement PoolStatusDisplay dashboard renderable","description":"## Purpose
Create a visual dashboard renderable that displays connection pool status at a glance, showing pool utilization, health, and queue status.

## Background
Connection pools are critical infrastructure. Operators need to quickly assess:
- How many connections are active vs idle
- Whether the pool is under pressure (requests waiting)
- Health metrics (timeouts, errors)
- Configuration (min/max connections)

## Implementation Details

### File Location
crates/sqlmodel-console/src/renderables/pool_status.rs

### Core Struct
PoolStatusDisplay struct holds:
- stats: PoolStats (from sqlmodel-pool)
- theme: Theme
- width: Option<usize>
- show_history: bool (show recent utilization trend)

### PoolStats (expected interface from sqlmodel-pool)
struct PoolStats {
    active_connections: u32,
    idle_connections: u32,
    waiting_requests: u32,
    max_connections: u32,
    min_connections: u32,
    total_acquired: u64,
    total_released: u64,
    avg_acquisition_time_ms: f64,
    pool_created_at: Instant,
}

### Visual Layout (Rich Mode)


### Health Status Logic
- HEALTHY: waiting=0, active < 80% of max
- BUSY: waiting=0, active >= 80% of max  
- DEGRADED: waiting > 0 but < max
- EXHAUSTED: waiting >= max or active = max for extended period

### Color Coding
- Healthy: green
- Busy: yellow
- Degraded: orange
- Exhausted: red

### Plain Text (Agent Mode)
Pool: 8/20 active (40%), 0 waiting, HEALTHY
  Active: 5, Idle: 3, Max: 20
  Avg acquire: 2.3ms, Uptime: 2h 15m

## API Design
PoolStatusDisplay::new(stats: &PoolStats)
  .theme(theme)
  .width(80)
  .show_history(true)
  .render(width) / .render_plain()

## Verification Steps
1. Test with healthy pool (low utilization)
2. Test with busy pool (high utilization)
3. Test with exhausted pool (waiting > 0)
4. Verify bar chart renders correctly at various widths
5. Verify plain text output
6. Test uptime formatting
7. Test with zero stats (fresh pool)

## Dependencies
- sqlmodel-pool for PoolStats interface
- rich_rust Panel, ProgressBar components
- Theme from this crate","acceptance_criteria":"PoolStatusDisplay shows active/idle/max connections
Display updates in real-time during pool activity
Display shows connection acquisition queue depth
Plain mode outputs status line format
All unit tests verify rendering
Performance tests confirm low overhead","notes":"PoolStatusDisplay dashboard fully implemented with PoolHealth enum, PoolStatsProvider trait, render_plain()/render_styled() methods, 23 tests passing, all quality gates pass","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:12:10.255536895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:53:10.887703018Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:53:10.887618990Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lv","depends_on_id":"bd-1q2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1lv","depends_on_id":"bd-1rn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"bd-1lv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests to Implement

1. test_pool_status_creation - verify construction
2. test_pool_status_healthy - verify healthy state styling
3. test_pool_status_busy - verify busy state styling
4. test_pool_status_degraded - verify degraded state styling
5. test_pool_status_exhausted - verify exhausted state styling
6. test_pool_utilization_bar - verify bar rendering
7. test_pool_render_plain - verify text format
8. test_pool_render_rich - verify panel output
9. test_pool_stats_formatting - verify number formatting
10. test_pool_uptime_display - verify duration formatting

Use MockPool from fixtures (bd-1pw).","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:27:53Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1m0","title":"Write comprehensive rustdoc for sqlmodel-console API","description":"## Purpose
Add thorough rustdoc documentation to all public types, traits, and functions in sqlmodel-console crate.

## Background
Rustdoc is the primary API reference for Rust libraries. Every public item needs:
- Purpose description
- Usage examples
- Parameter documentation
- Return value documentation
- Links to related items

## Implementation Details

### Documentation Scope
All public items in:
- lib.rs (crate overview, feature flags)
- console.rs (SqlModelConsole, OutputMode)
- theme.rs (Theme, color definitions)
- All renderables (ErrorPanel, QueryResultTable, etc.)
- Progress components
- Traits (ConsoleAware, etc.)

### Documentation Style

#### Module-level
//! # sqlmodel-console
//!
//! Rich terminal output for SQLModel Rust operations.
//!
//! ## Quick Start
//! (code example)
//!
//! ## Features
//! - Agent-safe output (auto-detects AI coding agents)
//! - Rich renderables (tables, panels, trees)
//! - Themeable colors
//!
//! ## Feature Flags
//! - console (default): Enable console output
//! - syntax: Enable SQL syntax highlighting

#### Struct Documentation
/// A styled error panel for displaying sqlmodel errors.
///
/// ErrorPanel renders errors with:
/// - Color-coded severity
/// - Error context and location
/// - Suggestions when available
///
/// # Examples
///
/// Basic usage:
/// (code example)
///
/// With custom theme:
/// (code example)
///
/// # Agent Compatibility
/// In plain mode, renders as: ERROR: message (code)
pub struct ErrorPanel { ... }

#### Method Documentation
/// Create a new error panel from an Error.
///
/// # Arguments
/// * error - The sqlmodel error to display
///
/// # Returns
/// A new ErrorPanel ready for rendering
///
/// # Example
/// (code example)
pub fn new(error: &Error) -> Self { ... }

### Cross-References
Use intra-doc links:
/// See also: [Theme::error_color]
/// Related: [QueryResultTable] for displaying results

### Feature-Gated Documentation
#[cfg_attr(doc, doc = "Requires feature: syntax")]
#[cfg(feature = "syntax")]
pub struct DdlDisplay { ... }

### Examples in Docs
All examples should:
- Compile (cargo test --doc)
- Be minimal but complete
- Show both rich and plain output where relevant

## Verification Steps
1. cargo doc builds without warnings
2. cargo test --doc passes
3. All public items have documentation
4. Examples are runnable
5. Cross-references resolve
6. Feature flags documented

## Dependencies
- All console APIs finalized
- Code examples working","acceptance_criteria":"All public types have rustdoc comments
All public functions have rustdoc comments with examples
Module-level documentation explains purpose
Examples in docs compile and run (doctests)
cargo doc generates without warnings
Documentation follows Rust API guidelines","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:16:58.274332388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T06:56:17.692485588Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T06:56:17.692361477Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1m0","depends_on_id":"bd-2e8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m0","depends_on_id":"bd-2sh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1sl","title":"Phase 3: Error Display System","description":"# Phase 3: Error Display System

## Overview

This phase implements beautiful error display throughout sqlmodel_rust. Well-formatted
errors are one of the most impactful features because they directly help developers
debug issues faster.

## Why Error Display Matters

Current state: Errors are plain text, often missing context.

```
Error: syntax error at position 7
```

With rich error display:

```
╭─────────────────────── SQL Syntax Error ───────────────────────╮
│                                                                 │
│  Unexpected token 'SELCT' near position 7                      │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─ Query ──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ SELCT * FROM users WHERE id = $1                         │  │
│  │ ^^^^^                                                    │  │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                 │
│  💡 Hint: Did you mean 'SELECT'?                               │
│  SQLSTATE: 42601                                               │
│                                                                 │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

## Components to Implement

### 1. ErrorPanel Renderable
A panel specifically designed for errors with:
- Red border
- Error title based on error type
- Formatted message
- Optional SQL context with highlighting
- Optional hints
- SQLSTATE code display

### 2. Error Trait Extensions
Add methods to sqlmodel-core Error types:
- `to_panel()` → Rich panel representation
- `to_plain()` → Plain text representation
- `to_json()` → Structured JSON representation

### 3. QueryError Visualization
Special handling for QueryError which has:
- SQL statement (can be syntax highlighted)
- Error position (can be underlined)
- SQLSTATE code
- Detail and hint fields

### 4. Connection Error Display
Special handling showing:
- Host/port that failed
- Authentication details (redacted)
- Timeout information
- Retry suggestions

## Error Type Coverage

| Error Type | Rich Display |
|------------|--------------|
| Connection | Host, port, auth method, timeout |
| Query | SQL with position marker, SQLSTATE |
| Type | Expected vs actual type, value |
| Transaction | State, nested level |
| Protocol | Message type, raw bytes (redacted) |
| Pool | Pool stats, wait time |
| Schema | Table/column info, constraint |
| Config | Setting name, value, valid range |
| Io | Path, operation, errno |
| Timeout | Duration, operation |
| Cancelled | Reason, duration |

## Design Principles

### Actionable Information
Every error panel should help the user understand:
1. What went wrong
2. Where it went wrong
3. Why it went wrong (if known)
4. How to fix it (if possible)

### Privacy Aware
- Passwords are never shown
- Connection strings are redacted
- Query parameters may contain sensitive data (redact or truncate)

### Plain Mode Parity
Plain mode output must contain all the same information, just without styling.
Use indentation and ASCII art for structure.

## Tasks in This Phase

1. Create ErrorPanel renderable in sqlmodel-console
2. Implement SqlModelError trait for mode-aware display
3. Extend sqlmodel-core Error with display methods
4. Implement QueryError visualization with SQL context
5. Implement ConnectionError visualization
6. Add error examples/demos

## Dependencies (Beads)

This phase depends on Phase 2 (Core Infrastructure) being complete.
The SqlModelConsole must exist before we can add error display.","acceptance_criteria":"ErrorPanel renderable displays errors beautifully in Rich mode
ErrorPanel shows clean text output in Plain mode
Error context (file, line, query) is displayed when available
Error suggestions are displayed when available
ErrorPanel integrates with existing sqlmodel Error type
All unit tests pass for error display
Visual regression tests pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:06:16.159600096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:17:15.196705900Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:17:15.196656086Z","close_reason":"Phase complete: ErrorPanel implemented with all rendering modes (Rich/Plain/JSON), SQL position markers, hints, SQLSTATE display, and 24 unit tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["errors","phase-3","rich-rust"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1sl","depends_on_id":"bd-1ob","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1sl","depends_on_id":"bd-1rn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1sl","depends_on_id":"bd-eqb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"bd-1sl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ErrorPanel renderable with severity, title, message, SQL context
- [ ] ErrorPanel renders correctly in Rich mode (Panel with border)

 {"id":"bd-1sw","title":"Create comprehensive e2e test suite with detailed logging","description":"## Purpose
Create a comprehensive end-to-end test suite that validates the entire console system working together, with detailed logging for debugging test failures.

## Background
Unit tests verify individual components. E2E tests verify:
- Components work together correctly
- Real terminal output matches expectations
- Agent mode produces parseable output
- Rich mode produces valid ANSI
- Feature flags work correctly in combination
- Performance meets requirements

## Implementation Details

### File Structure
```
crates/sqlmodel-console/
├── tests/
│   ├── e2e/
│   │   ├── mod.rs
│   │   ├── output_capture.rs      # Test utilities
│   │   ├── mode_switching.rs      # Mode detection e2e
│   │   ├── error_display.rs       # Error panel e2e
│   │   ├── query_results.rs       # Query table e2e
│   │   ├── progress_tracking.rs   # Progress components e2e
│   │   ├── agent_compat.rs        # Agent compatibility e2e
│   │   └── full_workflow.rs       # Complete workflows
│   └── fixtures/
│       ├── mod.rs
│       ├── sample_data.rs         # Test data generators
│       └── expected_output/       # Golden files
```

### Test Utilities (output_capture.rs)
```rust
//\! Utilities for capturing and analyzing console output in tests.

use std::io::{self, Write};
use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
use tracing::{info, debug};

/// Captured output from a test run.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct CapturedOutput {
    pub stdout: String,
    pub stderr: String,
    pub logs: Vec<String>,
    pub duration_ms: u64,
}

impl CapturedOutput {
    /// Check if stdout contains ANSI escape codes.
    pub fn stdout_has_ansi(&self) -> bool {
        self.stdout.contains("\x1b[") || self.stdout.contains("\033[")
    }

    /// Check if stderr contains ANSI escape codes.
    pub fn stderr_has_ansi(&self) -> bool {
        self.stderr.contains("\x1b[") || self.stderr.contains("\033[")
    }

    /// Get stdout lines.
    pub fn stdout_lines(&self) -> Vec<&str> {
        self.stdout.lines().collect()
    }

    /// Get stderr lines.
    pub fn stderr_lines(&self) -> Vec<&str> {
        self.stderr.lines().collect()
    }

    /// Assert stdout matches expected (with logging on failure).
    pub fn assert_stdout_eq(&self, expected: &str) {
        if self.stdout \!= expected {
            info\!("STDOUT MISMATCH");
            info\!("Expected ({} bytes):", expected.len());
            for (i, line) in expected.lines().enumerate() {
                debug\!("  {:3}: {}", i + 1, line);
            }
            info\!("Actual ({} bytes):", self.stdout.len());
            for (i, line) in self.stdout.lines().enumerate() {
                debug\!("  {:3}: {}", i + 1, line);
            }
            panic\!("stdout mismatch");
        }
    }

    /// Assert stdout contains substring.
    pub fn assert_stdout_contains(&self, substring: &str) {
        if \!self.stdout.contains(substring) {
            info\!("STDOUT MISSING SUBSTRING: {}", substring);
            info\!("Full stdout:");
            for line in self.stdout.lines() {
                debug\!("  {}", line);
            }
            panic\!("stdout missing expected substring");
        }
    }

    /// Assert no ANSI codes in plain mode.
    pub fn assert_plain_mode_clean(&self) {
        if self.stdout_has_ansi() {
            info\!("ANSI codes found in stdout during plain mode\!");
            info\!("Offending bytes: {:?}", self.stdout.as_bytes());
            panic\!("plain mode should have no ANSI codes in stdout");
        }
    }
}

/// Capture stdout/stderr during a closure execution.
pub fn capture_output<F: FnOnce()>(f: F) -> CapturedOutput {
    use std::time::Instant;

    let start = Instant::now();

    // Use gag crate or similar for real capture
    // This is a simplified version
    let stdout_capture = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::new()));
    let stderr_capture = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::new()));

    // ... capture implementation

    f();

    let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;

    CapturedOutput {
        stdout: String::from_utf8_lossy(&stdout_capture.lock().unwrap()).to_string(),
        stderr: String::from_utf8_lossy(&stderr_capture.lock().unwrap()).to_string(),
        logs: Vec::new(), // Populated by tracing subscriber
        duration_ms,
    }
}
```

### Mode Switching E2E Tests (mode_switching.rs)
```rust
//\! E2E tests for output mode switching.

use super::output_capture::*;
use sqlmodel_console::{SqlModelConsole, OutputMode};
use tracing_test::traced_test;

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn e2e_plain_mode_produces_no_ansi() {
    let output = capture_output(|| {
        std::env::set_var("SQLMODEL_PLAIN", "1");
        let console = SqlModelConsole::new();

        console.print("Hello world");
        console.success("Operation complete");
        console.error("Something failed");
        console.rule(Some("Section"));

        std::env::remove_var("SQLMODEL_PLAIN");
    });

    output.assert_plain_mode_clean();
    assert\!(logs_contain("Output mode detection complete"));
    assert\!(logs_contain("detected_mode"));
}

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn e2e_agent_detection_claude_code() {
    let output = capture_output(|| {
        std::env::set_var("CLAUDE_CODE", "1");
        let console = SqlModelConsole::new();
        assert\!(console.is_plain());
        console.print("Agent output");
        std::env::remove_var("CLAUDE_CODE");
    });

    output.assert_plain_mode_clean();
}

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn e2e_agent_detection_codex_cli() {
    let output = capture_output(|| {
        std::env::set_var("CODEX_CLI", "1");
        let console = SqlModelConsole::new();
        assert\!(console.is_plain());
        std::env::remove_var("CODEX_CLI");
    });

    output.assert_plain_mode_clean();
}

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn e2e_force_rich_overrides_agent() {
    let output = capture_output(|| {
        std::env::set_var("CLAUDE_CODE", "1");
        std::env::set_var("SQLMODEL_RICH", "1");
        let console = SqlModelConsole::new();
        assert\!(console.is_rich());
        std::env::remove_var("CLAUDE_CODE");
        std::env::remove_var("SQLMODEL_RICH");
    });

    // Rich mode should have ANSI (if feature enabled)
    #[cfg(feature = "rich")]
    assert\!(output.stderr_has_ansi() || output.stdout_has_ansi());
}
```

### Full Workflow E2E Test (full_workflow.rs)
```rust
//\! Complete workflow E2E tests simulating real usage.

use super::*;
use sqlmodel_console::prelude::*;
use tracing::{info, span, Level};

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn e2e_complete_query_workflow() {
    let _span = span\!(Level::INFO, "e2e_complete_query_workflow").entered();

    info\!("Starting complete query workflow test");

    let output = capture_output(|| {
        std::env::set_var("SQLMODEL_PLAIN", "1");

        let console = SqlModelConsole::new();

        // 1. Show connection status
        console.status("Connecting to database...");
        info\!("Emitted connection status");

        // 2. Show query execution
        console.info("Executing: SELECT * FROM users LIMIT 10");

        // 3. Display results
        let columns = vec\!["id", "name", "email"];
        let rows = vec\![
            vec\!["1", "Alice", "alice@example.com"],
            vec\!["2", "Bob", "bob@example.com"],
        ];
        let table = QueryResultTable::new(columns, rows);
        console.print(&table.render_plain());

        // 4. Show completion
        console.success("Query returned 2 rows in 12ms");

        std::env::remove_var("SQLMODEL_PLAIN");
    });

    info\!("Test output captured: {} stdout bytes, {} stderr bytes",
          output.stdout.len(), output.stderr.len());

    // Verify output structure
    output.assert_stdout_contains("id");
    output.assert_stdout_contains("name");
    output.assert_stdout_contains("Alice");
    output.assert_stdout_contains("Bob");
    output.assert_plain_mode_clean();

    // Verify logs captured
    assert\!(logs_contain("Emitted connection status"));
    assert\!(logs_contain("e2e_complete_query_workflow"));

    info\!("E2E test complete: workflow passed");
}

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn e2e_error_handling_workflow() {
    let _span = span\!(Level::INFO, "e2e_error_handling_workflow").entered();

    let output = capture_output(|| {
        std::env::set_var("SQLMODEL_PLAIN", "1");

        let console = SqlModelConsole::new();

        // Simulate SQL error
        let error = ErrorPanel::new("SQL Syntax Error", "Unexpected token 'SELCT'")
            .with_sql("SELCT * FROM users")
            .with_position(1)
            .with_hint("Did you mean 'SELECT'?")
            .with_sqlstate("42601");

        console.print(&error.to_plain());

        std::env::remove_var("SQLMODEL_PLAIN");
    });

    output.assert_stdout_contains("SQL Syntax Error");
    output.assert_stdout_contains("SELCT");
    output.assert_stdout_contains("Hint:");
    output.assert_stdout_contains("SELECT");
    output.assert_plain_mode_clean();
}
```

### Running E2E Tests
```bash
# Run all e2e tests with logging
RUST_LOG=sqlmodel_console=debug cargo test -p sqlmodel-console e2e -- --nocapture

# Run specific e2e test with trace logging
RUST_LOG=sqlmodel_console=trace cargo test -p sqlmodel-console e2e::full_workflow -- --nocapture

# Run with feature combinations
cargo test -p sqlmodel-console --features rich e2e
cargo test -p sqlmodel-console --no-default-features e2e
```

 {"id":"bd-1vz","title":"Phase 1: Foundation - Create sqlmodel-console Crate","description":"# Phase 1: Foundation - Create sqlmodel-console Crate

## Overview

This phase establishes the foundational infrastructure for the rich_rust integration by 
creating a new crate `sqlmodel-console` that will serve as the central coordination point
for all console output throughout sqlmodel_rust.

## Why a Separate Crate?

1. **Optional Dependency**: By isolating rich_rust behind a crate with feature flags, 
   users who don't need fancy output pay zero cost (no additional dependencies).

2. **Single Responsibility**: Console output concerns are separated from database logic.

3. **Consistent API**: All crates use the same SqlModelConsole interface.

4. **Testing Isolation**: Console tests don't pollute database crate tests.

## Crate Structure

```
crates/sqlmodel-console/
├── Cargo.toml              # Feature-gated rich_rust dependency
└── src/
    ├── lib.rs              # Public API, module exports, prelude
    ├── mode.rs             # OutputMode enum and detection logic
    ├── console.rs          # SqlModelConsole struct - main coordinator
    ├── theme.rs            # Color theme definitions
    ├── renderables/        # SQLModel-specific renderables
    │   └── mod.rs          # (populated in later phases)
    └── widgets/            # Progress bars, spinners
        └── mod.rs          # (populated in later phases)
```

## Key Design Decisions

### Feature Flags
```toml
[features]
default = []                  # No rich output by default
rich = ["rich_rust"]          # Basic rich output
syntax = ["rich", "rich_rust/syntax"]  # + SQL highlighting
full = ["rich", "syntax"]     # All features
```

### Conditional Compilation
All rich_rust types are behind `#[cfg(feature = "rich")]` so the crate compiles
cleanly even without the dependency. Plain mode is always available.

### Path Dependency
rich_rust is referenced as: `{ path = "../../../rich_rust", optional = true }`
This avoids crates.io publication issues and keeps the projects in sync.

## Tasks in This Phase

1. Create crate directory structure
2. Write Cargo.toml with proper feature flags
3. Create lib.rs with module declarations and prelude
4. Add crate to workspace Cargo.toml
5. Verify it builds with and without features

## Dependencies (External Crates)

- `rich_rust` (optional) - Terminal rendering
- `serde` - For theme serialization (optional)

## Verification Criteria

```bash
# Must pass:
cargo check -p sqlmodel-console
cargo check -p sqlmodel-console --features rich
cargo check -p sqlmodel-console --features full
cargo clippy -p sqlmodel-console --all-features -- -D warnings
```

## Notes for Implementer

- Follow the existing crate naming convention (sqlmodel-*)
- Use Rust 2024 edition like other crates
- Include standard #![forbid(unsafe_code)] as this is a pure Rust crate
- Add MIT/Apache-2.0 dual license header","acceptance_criteria":"Directory crates/sqlmodel-console/ exists with proper structure
Cargo.toml has correct feature flags (rich, syntax, full)
lib.rs has module declarations and #![forbid(unsafe_code)]
Crate is registered in workspace Cargo.toml
cargo check -p sqlmodel-console passes
cargo check -p sqlmodel-console --features full passes
cargo clippy -p sqlmodel-console --all-features passes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:02:33.929636709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:09:56.260411614Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T09:09:56.259746632Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","phase-1","rich-rust"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1vz","depends_on_id":"bd-eqb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"bd-1vz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-25i","title":"Implement OutputMode enum and detection logic","description":"# Implement OutputMode Enum and Detection Logic

## Task Description

Implement the core OutputMode enum and the detection function that determines
which mode to use based on environment variables and terminal state.

## File: src/mode.rs

```rust
//! Output mode detection for agent-safe console output.
//!
//! This module provides automatic detection of whether output should be
//! plain text (for AI agents and CI) or richly formatted (for humans).

use std::env;
use std::io::IsTerminal;

/// Output mode for console rendering.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
pub enum OutputMode {
    /// Plain text output, no ANSI codes. Machine-parseable.
    /// Used for: AI agents, CI, piped output, dumb terminals.
    Plain,
    
    /// Rich formatted output with colors, tables, panels.
    /// Used for: Interactive human terminal sessions.
    #[default]
    Rich,
    
    /// Structured JSON output for programmatic consumption.
    /// Used for: Tool integrations, scripting.
    Json,
}

impl OutputMode {
    /// Detect the appropriate output mode from environment.
    /// 
    /// Detection priority (first match wins):
    /// 1. SQLMODEL_PLAIN=1 → Plain
    /// 2. SQLMODEL_JSON=1 → Json  
    /// 3. SQLMODEL_RICH=1 → Rich (overrides agent detection!)
    /// 4. NO_COLOR=1 → Plain (standard env var)
    /// 5. CI=true → Plain
    /// 6. TERM=dumb → Plain
    /// 7. Agent env vars → Plain (see is_agent_environment)
    /// 8. !is_tty(stdout) → Plain
    /// 9. Default → Rich
    pub fn detect() -> Self {
        // Explicit overrides (highest priority)
        if env_is_truthy("SQLMODEL_PLAIN") {
            return Self::Plain;
        }
        if env_is_truthy("SQLMODEL_JSON") {
            return Self::Json;
        }
        if env_is_truthy("SQLMODEL_RICH") {
            return Self::Rich;  // Force rich even for agents
        }
        
        // Standard "no color" convention
        if env::var("NO_COLOR").is_ok() {
            return Self::Plain;
        }
        
        // CI environments
        if env_is_truthy("CI") {
            return Self::Plain;
        }
        
        // Dumb terminal
        if env::var("TERM").map(|t| t == "dumb").unwrap_or(false) {
            return Self::Plain;
        }
        
        // Agent detection
        if Self::is_agent_environment() {
            return Self::Plain;
        }
        
        // Not a TTY (piped, redirected)
        if !std::io::stdout().is_terminal() {
            return Self::Plain;
        }
        
        // Default: rich output for humans
        Self::Rich
    }
    
    /// Check if we're running in an AI coding agent environment.
    /// 
    /// Known agent environment variables:
    /// - CLAUDE_CODE: Claude Code CLI
    /// - CODEX_CLI: OpenAI Codex CLI
    /// - CURSOR_SESSION: Cursor IDE
    /// - AIDER_*: Aider coding assistant
    /// - AGENT_MODE: Generic agent marker
    /// - GITHUB_COPILOT_*: GitHub Copilot
    fn is_agent_environment() -> bool {
        let agent_markers = [
            "CLAUDE_CODE",
            "CODEX_CLI", 
            "CURSOR_SESSION",
            "AIDER_MODEL",
            "AIDER_REPO",
            "AGENT_MODE",
            "GITHUB_COPILOT",
            "CONTINUE_SESSION",  // Continue.dev
        ];
        
        agent_markers.iter().any(|var| env::var(var).is_ok())
    }
    
    /// Check if this mode should use ANSI codes.
    pub fn supports_ansi(&self) -> bool {
        matches!(self, Self::Rich)
    }
    
    /// Check if this mode should use structured format.
    pub fn is_structured(&self) -> bool {
        matches!(self, Self::Json)
    }
}

/// Check if an environment variable is set to a truthy value.
fn env_is_truthy(name: &str) -> bool {
    env::var(name)
        .map(|v| {
            let v = v.to_lowercase();
            v == "1" || v == "true" || v == "yes" || v == "on"
        })
        .unwrap_or(false)
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use std::env;
    
    // Helper to run test with clean environment
    fn with_clean_env<F: FnOnce()>(f: F) {
        // Save and clear relevant vars
        let vars_to_clear = [
            "SQLMODEL_PLAIN", "SQLMODEL_JSON", "SQLMODEL_RICH",
            "NO_COLOR", "CI", "CLAUDE_CODE", "CODEX_CLI",
        ];
        let saved: Vec<_> = vars_to_clear.iter()
            .map(|&v| (v, env::var(v).ok()))
            .collect();
        
        for &var in &vars_to_clear {
            env::remove_var(var);
        }
        
        f();
        
        // Restore
        for (var, val) in saved {
            match val {
                Some(v) => env::set_var(var, v),
                None => env::remove_var(var),
            }
        }
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_explicit_plain_override() {
        with_clean_env(|| {
            env::set_var("SQLMODEL_PLAIN", "1");
            assert_eq!(OutputMode::detect(), OutputMode::Plain);
        });
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_agent_detection() {
        with_clean_env(|| {
            env::set_var("CLAUDE_CODE", "1");
            assert_eq!(OutputMode::detect(), OutputMode::Plain);
        });
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_rich_override_beats_agent() {
        with_clean_env(|| {
            env::set_var("CLAUDE_CODE", "1");
            env::set_var("SQLMODEL_RICH", "1");
            assert_eq!(OutputMode::detect(), OutputMode::Rich);
        });
    }
}
```

## Key Implementation Details

### Why `is_agent_environment()` Is Separate
This function can be extended as new AI coding tools emerge. Having it as a 
separate function makes maintenance easier.

### Environment Variable Detection
We check for existence (`is_ok()`) rather than specific values for agent markers
because different tools may set them to different values. The presence of the
variable is the signal.

### Test Isolation
Tests manipulate environment variables, so they need careful isolation to avoid
affecting other tests or the system. The `with_clean_env` helper handles this.

## Verification

```bash
# Unit tests
cargo test -p sqlmodel-console mode::tests

# Manual testing
SQLMODEL_PLAIN=1 cargo run --example mode_check
CLAUDE_CODE=1 cargo run --example mode_check
cargo run --example mode_check  # Should be Rich if in terminal
```","acceptance_criteria":"OutputMode enum has Plain, Rich, Json variants
detect() function checks all environment variables in correct priority
Agent detection covers Claude Code, Codex CLI, Cursor, Aider, Copilot
CI environment detection works for major CI systems
All unit tests pass including edge cases
Documentation includes detection priority order","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:04:27.538848161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:06:28.347316301Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T09:06:28.347211444Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mode","phase-2","rich-rust"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-25i","depends_on_id":"bd-1ob","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28n","title":"Integrate console into Session and Connection builders","description":"## Purpose
Extend Session and Connection builder APIs to accept console configuration, allowing users to easily attach console output to their database sessions.

## Background
The builder pattern is central to sqlmodel API. Users should be able to:
Session::builder()
    .with_console(console)
    .connect(url)
    .await

This makes console integration feel natural and idiomatic.

## Implementation Details

### File Modifications
crates/sqlmodel/src/session.rs
crates/sqlmodel/src/builder.rs (if exists)

### Builder Extensions

#### SessionBuilder
impl SessionBuilder {
    /// Attach a console for rich output
    #[cfg(feature = "console")]
    pub fn with_console(mut self, console: SqlModelConsole) -> Self {
        self.console = Some(Arc::new(console));
        self
    }

    /// Attach a shared console
    #[cfg(feature = "console")]
    pub fn with_shared_console(mut self, console: Arc<SqlModelConsole>) -> Self {
        self.console = Some(console);
        self
    }

    /// Use auto-detected output mode
    #[cfg(feature = "console")]
    pub fn with_auto_console(mut self) -> Self {
        self.console = Some(Arc::new(SqlModelConsole::auto()));
        self
    }
}

#### ConnectionBuilder (for each driver)
Similar pattern for driver-specific connection builders.

### Console Propagation
When Session is built:
1. If console is set, propagate to underlying connection
2. Connection implements ConsoleAware, receives console
3. All operations can now emit console output

### Without Console Feature
When console feature is disabled:
- with_console methods do not exist (compile error if used)
- No console field in builders
- Zero overhead

## Usage Examples

### Explicit Console
let console = SqlModelConsole::builder()
    .theme(Theme::dark())
    .build();

let session = Session::builder()
    .with_console(console)
    .connect("postgres://...")
    .await?;

### Auto Console
let session = Session::builder()
    .with_auto_console()
    .connect("postgres://...")
    .await?;

### Shared Console (multiple sessions)
let console = Arc::new(SqlModelConsole::auto());
let session1 = Session::builder()
    .with_shared_console(console.clone())
    .connect(url1).await?;
let session2 = Session::builder()
    .with_shared_console(console)
    .connect(url2).await?;

## Verification Steps
1. Builder compiles with console feature
2. Builder compiles without console feature
3. Console propagates to connection
4. Operations emit output when console attached
5. No output when console not attached
6. Shared console works across sessions

## Dependencies
- SqlModelConsole and ConsoleAware trait
- Session and Connection implementations","acceptance_criteria":"Session builder accepts optional console parameter

 {"id":"bd-2e8","title":"Create unit test suite for sqlmodel-console","description":"## Purpose
Implement comprehensive unit tests for all sqlmodel-console modules, ensuring logic correctness and edge case handling.

## Background
Unit tests verify individual components in isolation. Each module needs tests for:
- Normal operation
- Edge cases (empty inputs, boundaries)
- Error conditions
- Configuration variations

## Implementation Details

### Test File Locations
crates/sqlmodel-console/src/mode.rs -> tests at module level
crates/sqlmodel-console/src/theme.rs -> tests at module level
crates/sqlmodel-console/tests/ -> integration-style unit tests

### Mode Detection Tests
#[test]
fn test_detects_claude_code() {
    std::env::set_var("CLAUDE_CODE", "1");
    assert_eq!(OutputMode::detect(), OutputMode::Plain);
    std::env::remove_var("CLAUDE_CODE");
}

#[test]
fn test_detects_human_terminal() {
    // With no agent env vars and a TTY
    assert_eq!(OutputMode::detect(), OutputMode::Rich);
}

#[test]
fn test_force_plain_override() {
    std::env::set_var("SQLMODEL_PLAIN", "1");
    assert_eq!(OutputMode::detect(), OutputMode::Plain);
}

### Theme Tests
#[test]
fn test_default_theme_colors() {
    let theme = Theme::default();
    assert!(theme.error_color().is_red_variant());
    assert!(theme.success_color().is_green_variant());
}

#[test]
fn test_theme_parse_from_string() {
    let theme = Theme::from_name("dark");
    assert!(theme.is_ok());
}

#[test]
fn test_custom_theme_builder() {
    let theme = Theme::builder()
        .error_color(Color::Rgb(255, 0, 0))
        .build();
    // verify customization applied
}

### Renderable Tests
#[test]
fn test_error_panel_render() {
    let panel = ErrorPanel::new(Error::connection_failed("timeout"));
    let segments = panel.render(80);
    assert!(!segments.is_empty());
}

#[test]
fn test_error_panel_plain() {
    let panel = ErrorPanel::new(Error::connection_failed("timeout"));
    let text = panel.render_plain();
    assert!(text.contains("connection"));
    assert!(text.contains("timeout"));
}

#[test]
fn test_query_result_table_empty() {
    let table = QueryResultTable::new(vec![], vec![]);
    let text = table.render_plain();
    assert!(text.contains("0 rows") || text.is_empty());
}

### Progress Tests
#[test]
fn test_progress_percentage() {
    let p = OperationProgress::new("test", 100).completed(50);
    assert_eq!(p.percentage(), 50.0);
}

#[test]
fn test_progress_eta_calculation() {
    let mut p = OperationProgress::new("test", 100);
    // Simulate some elapsed time
    p.completed(50);
    // ETA should be roughly equal to elapsed time
}

## Test Categories
1. Construction tests - verify objects build correctly
2. Rendering tests - verify output generation
3. Plain text tests - verify agent-safe output
4. Edge cases - empty data, very large data, special characters
5. Thread safety - concurrent access patterns

## Verification Steps
1. All tests pass with cargo test
2. No test flakiness
3. Coverage report shows >80% for console crate
4. Tests run in <10 seconds

## Dependencies
- All console modules implemented
- Test fixtures/helpers defined","acceptance_criteria":"Unit tests cover all public API surface

Tests verify both Rich and Plain modes
